pos·it ˈpɒz.ɪt ˈpɑː.zɪt
verb
posit (SUGGEST)
C2
[T]
to suggest that something is true
Scientists posit that the universe began with a big bang.
She posited a connection between the two events.
noun
a statement or idea put forward as a basis for discussion
His posit was rejected by the committee.
These findings posit a new direction for research in this field. Cambridge English Corpus
posit verb noun
position noun verb
positive adjective noun
